musicalLanguage.description: Stevie Wonder's Ribbon in the Sky appears as a 61-measure transcription at 65 BPM with 3,941 notes, and its repetition data comes back strikingly empty — no measure ever repeats byte-identically and the pattern lens finds only short, weak-graded cells, so the piece reads as continuously through-varied. Harmony stays almost entirely unlabeled: exactly one window clears the confidence gate, an implied G# major at measure 59 — Ab in flats, the IV of the stated Eb major — arriving inside the closing region; whole-song detection is pure noise (0.02 margin), so the stated Eb major (0.348 fit) stands unconfirmed and uncontradicted. What the data states loudly is one bar: measure 28 carries 68 onsets — far past the next-busiest (49 at measure 56) — and simultaneously contains the right hand's ceiling, B6, with a single three-note shape flung across transpositions spanning two octaves (+23, +18, +11, +6, and -1 semitones) inside that one measure. Density and register peak together, once. The left hand reaches G1 at measure 44; the file opens from silence (measures 1-2 empty) and ends in it (measure 61).
musicalLanguage.structure: Seven regions. The music enters over near-silence — measures 1-2 carry nothing in the right hand and the left hand rests through measure 3 — and the lens fires its largest boundary at measure 5 (novelty 7.913). Interior turns arrive at measures 28 (2.232, the great density spike), 36 (2.888), and 57 (3.816, opening the close where the lone implied G# major window lands at measure 59). With no literal repeats and only weak short cells, the form here is carried by density and register: a rise through the 20s to the 68-onset outlier at measure 28, a second crest at measure 56 (49 onsets), then a five-measure close that thins to 4 onsets at measure 60 and written silence at measure 61.
keyMoments: Measure 28 is the piece in miniature: 68 onsets — far beyond the next-busiest bar (49 at measure 56) — plus the right hand's ceiling B6, plus one three-note shape recurring inside the bar at transpositions of +23, +18, +11, +6, and -1 semitones: a written-out cadenza sweep across two octaves.

Exactly one chord window clears the confidence gate in 61 measures: an implied G# major at measure 59 — read in flats, Ab, the IV of the stated Eb major — landing inside the closing region the lens opens at measure 57 (novelty 3.816).

Nothing repeats literally in the whole file — zero byte-identical measures, and only weak short pattern cells — so every phrase is a fresh voicing of the material: the opposite of vamp-based writing, and a real reading challenge at 65 BPM.

The frame is silence: the right hand rests through measures 1-2, the left through measures 1-3, and the final measure 61 is empty — the music materializes and dissolves rather than starting and stopping.
teachingGoals: Practice measure 28 as its own project: 68 onsets with a two-octave transposition sweep and the B6 ceiling in one bar — isolate it, build it slowly, and only then place it back into the phrase.
Learn the density arc as the form: rise to measure 28, second crest at measure 56 (49 onsets), release to 4 onsets at measure 60 — shape your dynamics along that measured curve.

Develop ear-first harmony here — with one implied label (G# major, measure 59) in the whole file and detection at noise level, listen for the bass's motion and name the changes yourself; the data honestly cannot.

Recognize the through-varied design: since no measure returns literally, practice by section (the lens's regions at measures 5, 18, 28, 36, 45, and 57) rather than hunting for repeats that are not there.
styleTips: Lay the melody behind the beat once it is learned — at 65 BPM this style breathes late against a steady pulse; listen for the vocal-style lag while the left hand keeps honest time.
Play measure 28's sweep as one gesture, not 68 events — think of the two-octave rise toward B6 as a single arc and listen for its top note floating rather than banging.

Keep the written silences real: enter from the empty measures 1-2 as if the sound was already ringing somewhere, and let measure 61's silence finish the piece — hands still, pedal down until the decay ends.
Imitate a reference recording by ear for the ornamental turns — a through-varied file like this one is a transcription of a performance tradition, and the records carry the feel the grid cannot.
